description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages QA, Deploy and Release using the ${BUILDER_CONTAINER}:master Docker image for most jobs. Add a "Build and test" job in the QA stage that runs `docker build --pull -t "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}" .` then `docker run --rm "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}" prove -lv t`, and triggers on any commit that is not on master, production, preprod, development and not a release tag. Add a "REUSE Compliance" job in QA that uses the fsfe/reuse:latest image (entrypoint cleared) and runs `reuse lint` on every pipeline. Add a "Build and deploy to platform" job in the Deploy stage that first logs in to the GitLab Container Registry with `docker login -u "$CI_REGISTRY_USER" -p "$CI_BUILD_TOKEN" "$CI_REGISTRY"`, builds the image with `docker build --pull -t "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}" .`, then pushes it using `docker push ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}` and copies it to a CI‑specific tag with `skopeo copy --all ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG} ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}-ci-${CI_PIPELINE_IID}`, running only on master, production, preprod, development branches or on tags matching release/*. Add a "Push to AWS ACR" job in the Release stage that logs in to AWS ECR via `$(aws ecr get-login --no-include-email --region "$AWS_ECR_REGION")`, builds `docker build -t ${ECR_REPOSITORY_URL}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG} .` and pushes with `docker push ${ECR_REPOSITORY_URL}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}`, executing only when ECR_REPOSITORY_URL is set, not a merge request, and the commit tag matches release/*. Add a "Push to Gitlab Registry" job in Release that pulls the image `docker pull ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}`, logs in to the registry, tags it with `docker tag ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}} ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}` and pushes with `docker push ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}`, with the same rules as the AWS job.
